name: Toasted Pinon Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup pinon nuts or pine nuts (available in bulk at health food stores)
Ice cream topping of your choice -- hot fudge, caramel, or butterscotch, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 250 degrees.
2. Spread the nuts on a baking sheet and toast in the oven, stirring frequently, until pale brown (8-10 minutes). Avoid burning them; remove when toasted but not too dark, as they will continue cooking as they cool.
3. Put cream in a large bowl and whip until stiff. Cover and refrigerate.
4. In a bowl, combine egg yolks, cinnamon, and vanilla extract. Whip on medium speed until light yellow and thickened (about 5 minutes). Set aside.
5. In a small, heavy-bottomed saucepan, combine sugar and 1 1/4 cups water. Bring to a boil over high heat, stirring constantly; stop stirring when boiling to prevent crystallization. Continue cooking without stirring until caramelized and deep amber (20-25 minutes).
6. Remove from heat and gradually add the remaining 2 tablespoons water with a whisk until smooth. Protect hands and arms as syrup may splatter.
7. With mixer on high speed, slowly drizzle hot syrup into yolk mixture. Ensure mixture reaches at least 160 degrees using an instant-read thermometer, then beat until cooled to room temperature and thickened like whipped cream.
8. Gently fold the syrup/yolk mixture into whipped cream and stir in nuts. Place in a freezer container, cover tightly, and freeze for 6-8 hours before serving. Top with desired ice cream topping.
Makes 6-8 servings
